project: Model Atlas — Phase 1 Fresh CI and Smoke Tests
description: Harden Phase 1 quality gates so make ci passes from a fresh checkout, wire baseline GitHub Actions with Bun lockfile install, extend registry validation and route smoke tests with direct behavioral evidence, and resolve or document the Next.js Turbopack filesystem tracing warning.

context.customerAsk: Phase 1 completion and architectural-checklist hardening: make the quality gates reliable from a fresh checkout and wire baseline CI. Ensure Makefile/package scripts generate Fumadocs source before standalone typecheck when needed, keep make ci as lint, typecheck, test, build, and validate-data, and add a GitHub Actions workflow that installs with Bun's committed lockfile and runs make ci. Add or extend smoke tests that assert the Phase 1 route/content surface exists and that registry validation covers messages, assets, related docs, tags, and citations. Address the current Next/Turbopack filesystem tracing warning if it can be fixed by scoping content loaders cleanly; otherwise document the residual warning in progress or a technical note.
context.problem: Fresh clones fail typecheck without manually running fumadocs-mdx because .source/ is gitignored; GitHub Actions CI is missing; registry validation lacks complete negative test coverage for related docs and tags; and next build emits a Turbopack NFT filesystem tracing warning through content loader import chains.
context.solution: Add pretypecheck Fumadocs generation, preserve make ci as lint/typecheck/test/build/validate-data, add a GitHub Actions workflow running make ci with bun install --frozen-lockfile, extend registry validation and Phase 1 smoke tests with behavioral failure cases, and scope content loaders or document the residual Turbopack warning.

acceptanceCriteria: After bun install on a fresh clone with no .source/, make typecheck and make ci exit 0 without manual codegen.
make ci runs lint, typecheck, test, build with Phase 1 static route verification, and validate-data in that order.
GitHub Actions installs with the committed bun.lock using frozen lockfile semantics and runs make ci on pull requests and pushes to main.
Registry validation tests demonstrate failure for broken messages, assets, relatedIds, tags, and citations, and success on the Phase 1 baseline.
Smoke tests render Phase 1 discovery routes and sample docs pages; build route verification fails when a required Phase 1 static route is missing.
next build produces no Turbopack NFT filesystem-tracing warning, or the residual warning is documented in progress.txt with trace and mitigation notes.
Typecheck, lint, and tests pass via make ci.

userStories.title: Generate Fumadocs source before standalone typecheck
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need Fumadocs MDX source generated automatically before typecheck so fresh checkouts do not fail on missing .source/server.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json runs fumadocs-mdx before tsc --noEmit via pretypecheck or equivalent so src/lib/source.ts resolves without manual codegen.
pretest continues to run fumadocs-mdx so tests remain valid when typecheck is skipped.
Deleting .source/ then running make typecheck exits 0 after bun install.
README Quality Gates section documents Fumadocs source generation before typecheck and lists full make ci steps including validate-data.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extend registry validation negative coverage
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I need deterministic tests proving make validate-data catches broken messages, assets, related-document references, tags, and citations.
acceptanceCriteria: A Bun test fails validation when a registry record references a missing relatedIds target, naming the record id and missing related id.
A Bun test fails validation when a registry record references an unknown tag, naming the record and tag reference.
Tests continue to cover unresolved citationIds, missing colocated message keys, and missing asset message keys; committed Phase 1 baseline still passes.
Running make validate-data on the committed tree exits 0.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Strengthen Phase 1 route and content smoke tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated smoke tests that prove the Phase 1 browsing and sample content surface renders and build output includes required static routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery smoke tests render /, /search, /docs/architecture, /docs/glossary, /tags, /tags/attention, /docs/glossary/token, and /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention without throw and assert representative copy or canonical hrefs.
A test exercises scripts/verify-phase-1-static-routes.ts against a fixture manifest and fails when a required Phase 1 route is absent.
Tag browse helper smoke asserts grouped-query attention appears under the attention tag for Phase 1 baseline content.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add baseline GitHub Actions CI workflow
userStories.description: As a repository maintainer, I want pull requests enforced by automated CI running the same make ci path as local development.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml triggers on pull requests and pushes to main.
The workflow uses oven-sh/setup-bun and installs with the committed bun.lock using frozen lockfile semantics.
The workflow runs make ci from the repository root and fails the job when any step exits non-zero.
The baseline workflow does not require secrets for lint, typecheck, test, build, and validate-data.
README notes that CI runs make ci via GitHub Actions.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolve or document Turbopack filesystem tracing warning
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the Next.js build free of unintended whole-project filesystem tracing warnings, or a clear record of why a residual warning remains.
acceptanceCriteria: Running bun run build no longer prints the Turbopack NFT whole-project tracing warning from content loader import chains, or progress.txt documents the residual warning with import trace, root cause, attempted fix, and follow-up recommendation.
If code changes are made, runtime filesystem loaders scope reads to src/content or an equivalent static subfolder rather than open-ended process.cwd() traversal.
Grouped-query attention and token glossary pages still render successfully after any loader scoping change.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass when loader scoping behavior changes.
